D1 Optics L4 Analyze Reference

Fermat's Principle of Least Time

drag the refraction point across the water surface and find the path light actually takes

Among all paths from A to B, light follows the one that takes the least time — and Snell's law falls out as a direct consequence.

Slide the crossing point. The bottom plot shows the total time as a function of x — the gold curve marks the minimum, which exactly satisfies Snell's law.
